reasoning: 11.6 AdjEM gap with Duke's 37.3 AdjEM being the best in the entire field. Duke's WAB of 13.7 dwarfs St. John's 7.3, confirming elite quality wins. St. John's archetype twin 2023 UConn (Champion) is intriguing but Duke's own archetypes (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Florida Champion) are equally deep-run caliber at a higher efficiency tier.

1.4 AdjEM gap is razor-thin but UConn's WAB of 8.8 vs MSU's 7.4 and Barthag of 0.952 vs 0.944 indicate a slightly higher quality tier. Both teams map to 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) as top archetype twin. UConn's two recent championships (2023, 2024) reflect a program with tournament DNA that the data alone doesn't fully capture. The slimmest of edges.

0.8 AdjEM gap technically favors Illinois, but Houston's WAB of 9.0 vs Illinois's 6.6 is the decisive metric — Houston has been dramatically better against quality opponents. Houston's Barthag of 0.971 edges Illinois's 0.968. Houston's AdjD of 89.5 (2nd nationally) will turn this into a defensive slugfest that favors their style. Houston's archetypes (2015 Wisconsin Runner-Up) have deeper tournament pedigree.

9.3 AdjEM gap is substantial at the Sweet 16 level. Arizona's AdjEM of 35.5 and WAB of 13.7 are among the best in the field. Archetype twins 2025 Florida (Champion) and 2012 Kentucky (Champion) signal the highest ceiling in the West. Arkansas's archetype twin 2016 Oklahoma (F4) shows they could compete, but the efficiency gap is too wide.

6.8 AdjEM gap with Purdue's 33.0 AdjEM substantially above Gonzaga's 26.3. Purdue's WAB of 9.2 vs Gonzaga's 7.4 and Barthag of 0.964 vs 0.944 confirm a clear quality tier difference. Gonzaga's archetypes (2025 Tennessee E8, 2018 Michigan Runner-Up) are strong but Purdue's archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) is literally themselves — a known tournament performer.

9.1 AdjEM gap with Michigan's 36.6 AdjEM ranking 2nd in the field. Michigan's WAB of 14.0 is the highest in the entire tournament and their Barthag of 0.980 is near-perfect. Archetype twin 2012 Kentucky (Champion, 0.943 similarity) is the gold standard. Texas Tech's elite defense (AdjD 93.6) will make this competitive but Michigan's offensive firepower is overwhelming.

4.7 AdjEM gap in Iowa State's favor with Barthag of 0.966 vs Virginia's 0.943. Iowa State's archetype twins (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Houston Runner-Up) both reached championship games. Virginia's WAB of 7.8 edges ISU's 7.4, but Iowa State's overall efficiency profile and deep-run archetypes carry the day.
